Off Calendar Apr-28-2017 Text Ruling

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-16-2017 Continued To Jul-10-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-29-2017 Continued To Jun-07-17 At 01:00 P.M. In Dept 210

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert V. Chin, of Law Offices of Robert V. Chin, Tel. 415.987.5561, for plaintiffs;
Nathan N. Wang, of Law Offices of Carbone, Smoke, Smith, Bent & Leonard, Tel. 510.267.7278, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT YUK-SING CHAN, SARAH DENG's MOTION FOR SUMMARY Adjudication on punitive damages is denied as to Mr. Chan and granted as to Ms.Deng. Mr. Chan fails to satisfy his initial summary adjudication burden and, even if he did, there is a triable issue whether he is liable for punitive damages due to the evidence submitted by plaintiff David Competente that at the time of the collision Mr. Chan knew he was intoxicated. This evidence is sufficient per Taylor v. Superior Court (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890 and Civil Code 3294(c)(1) to show despicable conduct carried on by Mr. Chan with a willful and conscious disregard for the safety of others, including Mr. Competente. Mr. Chan's reliance on Dawes v. Superior Court (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 82 and Gombos v. Ashe (1958) 158 Cal.App.2d 517 is misplaced since those cases discuss the law on punitive damages liability of an intoxicated driver that existed before Taylor changed that law. Ms. Deng satisfied her intitial summary adjudication burden and Mr. Competente fails to present any evidence creating a triable dispute that Ms. Deng is liable for punitive damages. Ms. Deng's special interrogatories 13-15 and 22-24 and others ask for facts showing how Ms. Deng maliciously entrusted her vehicle to Mr. Chan. Mr. Compentente's responses to those interrogatories are factually devoid. In opposition to this motion, Mr. Competente merely refers to Mr. Chan's admission that he was drunk and the post-incident arrest (Plaintiff's Opposition to Undisputed Material Facts 19 and 20), which does not show any malice on the part of Ms. Deng. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
